free_text: Isotopic production cross sections of the residual
nuclei in spallation reactions induced by 136Xe
projectiles on proton at 500 A*MeV.
A liquid hydrogen target with a thickness of
87.3 mg/cm**2. The liquefied hydrogen was encapsulated
in a Ti container (36.3 mg/cm**2) surrounded by several
layers of Mylar-Al (8.3 mg/cm**2) for thermal
isolation. In order to subtract the contribution of
reactions in these layers of matter, measurements were
repeated with the empty target container.
Heavy residues produced in the target were all
strongly forward focused due to the inverse reaction
kinematics.They were identified using the fragment
separator (FRS).
two position-sensitive plastic scintillators.
two multi-sampling ionization chambers MUSICs
The heavy-ion synchrotron SIS18.
The production cross sections of the residual nuclei
identified in this reaction were obtained by
normalizing the measured yields to the number of atoms
per surface unit in the target and the number of
projectile nuclei irradiating the target. The
production yields of these nuclei were determined from
their complete momentum distribution corrected by the
reactions in the target container and any other effect
altering the primary production due to the experimental
technique used in this work.
.The uncertainty in the beam intensity is 4%.
.The uncertainty in the thickness of the
hydrogen target is 3%.
.The total uncertainty -statistical+systematic
SB
